target.name: Netrasemi
target.founder: Jyothis Indirabhai, Deepa Geetha, Sreejith Varma

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Netrasemi is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Jyothis Indirabhai, Deepa Geetha, and Sreejith Varma, having raised ₹107 Cr (~$13M) to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 4 same-stage, same-sector private companies in Chips & Semiconductors, supplemented by reference to 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

The peer-median method is the primary applicable approach. Four Series A semiconductor companies—Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M)—yield a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring using the semiconductor sector's 24.2x median price-to-sales multiple cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue for the target.

The analysis supports a triangulated valuation range of $1.3B (low) to $1.7B (high), centered on $1.5B median. This reflects the peer-median method as the sole quantitative anchor. Key uncertainties include Netrasemi's relative technology maturity, time-to-revenue, and capital intensity versus the peer cohort, particularly given the wide dispersion ($500M–$5.0B) observed among same-stage comparables. Additional disclosure of revenue trajectory or customer traction would sharpen this range materially.
